channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wildfire in Fraser Canyon Produces Pyrocumulus Clouds Over British Columbia
item.description: Pyrocumulus clouds, formed by intense heat from wildfires, are developing above the Fraser Canyon region in British Columbia. These towering storm clouds, often associated with severe weather, indicat...

item.title: Upcoming Protests and Activities for Sona 2026 in the Philippines and Abroad
item.description: The article details the planned protests and activities related to the 2026 State of the Nation Address (SONA) in the Philippines and abroad. Organized by groups like BAYAN, Sulong UBC, and ALPAS Pili...

item.title: Government funds national hockey teams ahead of World Cup after financial concerns
item.description: Sport, arts and culture minister Gayton McKenzie has allocated R3 million to support the national men’s and women’s hockey teams for the upcoming FIH Hockey World Cup, co-hosted by Belgium and the Net...

item.title: Singida Black Stars Add Nigerian Defender to Squad
item.description: The Singida Black Stars, a prominent Tanzanian football team, have made a strategic move by signing Nigerian centre back Emmanuel Okoye to bolster their defensive line. This acquisition comes as the t...

item.title: Sky News Australia rebrands to News24 amid political and media shifts
item.description: Sky News Australia is rebranding to News24, marking a significant shift in its identity. The move comes as part of News Corp's strategy to retain the News24 brand name after its 10-year UK deal expire...

item.title: Parliament remains stalled for four consecutive days as Opposition demands Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation
item.description: The first four days of the Monsoon Session of Parliament have ended without any legislative business being conducted, signalling the possibility of a prolonged deadlock between the government and the ...

item.title: Advocates Evaluate Provisions of Anti-Disinformation Bill
item.description: The House of Representatives approved House Bill No. 9465, the Digital Media Anti-False Information Act, which aims to penalize intentional online falsehoods causing public harm or threatening nationa...

item.title: Salman Rushdie Testifies in Federal Trial of Man Charged with Terrorism Over 2022 Attack
item.description: Salman Rushdie, the British-Indian author, testified in a federal terrorism trial in Buffalo, New York, against Hadi Matar, a 28-year-old Lebanese-American accused of attempting to kill him in 2022. P...

item.title: Pakistan and Seven Nations Condemn Israeli Minister's Al Aqsa Incursion
item.description: Pakistan and seven other countries, including Egypt, Turkey, Indonesia, Jordan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE, jointly condemned Israeli Minister Itamar Ben Gvir's incursion into Al Aqsa Mosque in ...

item.title: Premium skincare brand launches summer discount on award-winning silver-powered products
item.description: The article discusses a premium skincare brand that has introduced a summer discount on its award-winning silver-powered products, which are marketed as effective solutions for combating summer breako...

item.title: Singida Black Stars Add Nigerian Centre Back to Strengthen Team
item.description: The Singida Black Stars, a Tanzanian football team, have officially signed Nigeria's centre back to bolster their squad ahead of upcoming matches. The move aims to enhance defensive stability and comp...

item.title: CJP says protest at Jantar Mantar will continue despite Sonam Wangchuk ending his 26-day hunger strike
item.description: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke said the organisation's peaceful protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar will continue even after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day indefinite hunger...

item.title: Kenyan Politicians Discuss Leadership Succession and Legal Cases Ahead of 2027 Election
item.description: The article highlights key political developments in Kenya, focusing on Rigathi Gachagua's succession plan for the Mount Kenya region if his impeachment stands. Gachagua, leader of the Democratic Conv...

item.title: Six children hospitalised after consuming cooldrink from Ottery spaza shop; investigation ongoing
item.description: Six children aged between six and 15 were hospitalised in Cape Town after allegedly consuming a cooldrink purchased from a spaza shop in Ottery. The family of the affected children alleges that the dr...

item.title: Madison Square Garden Temporarily Restricted Internal Surveillance During Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Wedding Events
item.description: According to WIRED, Madison Square Garden temporarily restricted access to its internal surveillance system during Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's July 2026 wedding rehearsal dinner and, reportedly, a...

item.title: US Imposes New Tariffs on Australia Amid Trade Tensions
item.description: The Trump administration has announced new tariffs on 60 of its trading partners, including Australia, as part of ongoing trade disputes. The decision, revealed on July 24, 2026, affects a range of go...

item.title: Afrobeats' Global Rise: Nigeria's Cultural Influence at 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: Afrobeats has become a major force for Nigeria globally, even though the Super Eagles didn't win the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The genre's influence was felt through collaborations like Burna Boy's anthem ...

item.title: AAFP advocates peace and unity for Tanzania's progress
item.description: The African Alliance for Freedom and Progress (AAFP) has issued a strong call for national unity and peaceful coexistence to accelerate Tanzania's development. In a recent statement, the organization ...

item.title: Seahawks Safety Nick Emmanwori to Undergo Ankle Surgery, Expected Back by Week 1
item.description: Seattle Seahawks safety Nick Emmanwori is set to undergo ankle surgery, which will place him on the physically unable to perform (PUP) list for training camp. Sources confirmed to ESPN that Emmanwori,...

item.title: Man stabs two New Yorkers in Upper West Side after shouting 'Allahu Akbar'
item.description: A man stabbed two individuals in the Upper West Side of New York City after shouting 'Allahu Akbar'. The incident occurred during a period of heightened tensions, with the victim being a visibly Jewis...
